definitions.technical: The legal standard under which courts evaluate whether a federal agency's rulemaking was reasonable. Under Motor Vehicle Manufacturers Association v. State Farm (1983), an agency must examine relevant data and articulate a satisfactory explanation for its action, including a rational connection between the facts and its policy choices.
definitions.lay: The test courts use to decide whether a government agency made a reasonable decision when creating a rule. The agency must show it looked at the evidence and explained why its rule makes sense.
definitions.parenthetical: (the judicial standard requiring agencies to demonstrate reasoned decision making based on relevant data)
definitions.legal_significance: Motor Vehicle Mfrs. Ass'n v. State Farm, 463 U.S. 29 (1983), established this standard. Agency rules that fail to address important aspects of a problem or offer explanations contradicted by the evidence are vulnerable to vacatur.
